name: What kind of evidence do I need for a work chemical exposure workers comp case in Boise?
description: Medical records, MSDS, coworker statements, and incident reports are essential.

mainEntity.name: What kind of evidence do I need for a work chemical exposure workers comp case in Boise?

acceptedAnswer.text: Medical records, MSDS, coworker statements, and incident reports are essential.
acceptedAnswer.description: **Collect medical records, exposure logs, coworker testimonies, and workplace safety documents.** In Idaho, workers' comp requires proving the exposure caused your injury via documentation like Material Safety Data Sheets (MSDS) and incident reports.[1][6] Key factors: clear job-related link, timely filing (1 year from injury), and no pre-existing conditions undermining causation. Photograph conditions, save PPE records, and get doctor statements; avoid discussing with insurers without advice. Idaho attorneys specialize in building strong evidence packages.
